caption: Phase 4b: combat is now resolved PER SOLDIER. As two infantry blocks clash, each engaged front-rank soldier strikes the nearest enemy within its weapon reach, rolling the model's opposed contest (skill vs. facing-gated active defence) and wounding a per-soldier health pool; a soldier whose health runs out dies and the formation re-packs around the survivors. Casualties now emerge from the geometry of who is in reach of whom and from each soldier's own health -- not a regiment damage formula. This is the first gameplay change of the individual-collision pillar (#164): it also unblocks the spear-vs-sword reach standoff (#240), where a longer weapon strikes foes who cannot strike back. Balance constants (damage scale, hit sharpness) are a first cut, tuned against playtests.
